LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS 2001 THAI STICK, INC. AND THAI STK, INC.'S MOTION FOR DEFAULT RELIEF IS GRANTED UNDER THE MANDATORY PROVISIONS OF CCP 473(B). DEFENDANTS' ATTORNEY'S AFFIDAVIT ATTESTS THAT DEFAULT WAS ENTERED DUE TO HIS MISTAKES IN NOT CHECKING THE DOCKET AND MONITORING THE ACTION DURING TALKS WITH PLAINTIFFS, WHEN HE KNEW ENGLISH WAS NOT HIS CLIENT'S FIRST LANGUAGE AND THE CLIENT THUS MIGHT NOT UNDERSTAND DOCUMENTS SERVED ON HIM. CALIFORNIA COURTS SPLIT ON WHETHER MANDATORY 473(B) RELIEF IS PROPER WHEN BOTH LAWYER AND CLIENT ERR. (MARTIN POTTS & ASSOC. V. CORSAIR (2016) 244 CAL.APP.4TH 432, 442.) BUT PUBLIC POLICY FAVORS DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS. (RAPPLEYEA V. CAMPBELL (1994) 8 CAL.4TH 975, 980.) DEFENDANTS' REQUESTS FOR CCP 473(B) DISCRETIONARY RELIEF ARE DENIED AS UNTIMELY. (EDMON & KARNOW, CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL (TRG 2016) 5:366.) DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL SHALL PAY PLAINTIFFS' ATTORNEY FEES OF $2,946. WHEN 473(B) RELIEF IS GRANTED BASED ON AN ATTORNEY AFFIDAVIT, THE ATTORNEY MUST "PAY REASONABLE COMPENSATORY LEGAL FEES AND COSTS." (ID.) PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST THAT RELIEF BE CONDITIONED ON PAYMENT IS BARRED BY 473(C)(2). COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT REPEATS THE COURT'S RULING VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO, CSR#8600, E-MAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Default Judgment By Court; Request For Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Sep-06-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Prove-Up Hearing as to defaulted defendant 2001 Thai Stick Inc., a California corporation.

Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:52:41 a.m., adjourning at 11:07:53 a.m. and reconvening at 11:50 a.m.

Sean Tamura-Sato, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Watchanaporn Changto, Saksee Isanpayu, Arunroj Wiboon, Akaraphon Sodsaengsuk, Jattalee Chalernhinthong and Warittha Rasanakorn, who were not present.
Mr. Tamura-Sato appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant 2001 Thai Stick Inc., a California corporation.
Order waiving appearance of plaintiffs Watchanaporn Changto, Saksee Isanpayu, Arunroj Wiboon, Akaraphon Sodsaengsuk, Jattalee Chalernhinthong and Warittha Rasanakorn at default prove-up hearing was filed July 27, 2022.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract was filed on June 21, 2018, and personally served on defendants 2001 Thai Stick Inc., Thai STK, Inc. and Puasak Polemahasuppole on August 8, 2028.
Default as to all three defendants was entered on September 17, 2019.
Answer to plaintiffs' complaint by defendants 2001 Thai Stick Inc. and Thai STK, Inc. was filed on March 25, 2019.
Order STRIKING defendants 2001 Thai Stick Inc. and Thai STK, Inc. answer to complaint was filed with the Clerk's Office on May 2, 2019.
DOE defendants were dismissed on July 18, 2019.
Order GRANTING defendants 2001 Thai Stick Inc. and Thai STK, Inc.'s motion to set-aside defaults was filed November 6, 2019.

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS 2001 THAI STICK, INC. AND THAI STK, INC.'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants 2001 Thai Stick, Inc. and Thai Stk, Inc.'s motion for default relief is granted under the mandatory provisions of CCP 473(b). Defendants' attorney's affidavit attests that default was entered due to his mistakes in not checking the docket and monitoring the action during talks with plaintiffs, when he knew English was not his client's first language and the client thus might not understand documents served on him.

California courts split on whether mandatory 473(b) relief is proper when both lawyer and client err. (Martin Potts & Assoc. v. Corsair (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 432, 442.) But public policy favors deciding cases on their merits. (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 980.)

Defendants' requests for CCP 473(b) discretionary relief are denied as untimely. (Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 5:366.)

Defendants' counsel shall pay plaintiffs' attorney fees of $2,946. When 473(b) relief is granted based on an attorney affidavit, the attorney must "pay reasonable compensatory legal fees and costs." (Id.) Plaintiffs' request that relief be conditioned on payment is barred by 473(c)(2).

Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare and submit a proposed order that repeats the court's ruling verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sean Tamura-Sato, Esq., Minami Tamaki LLP, Telephone 415-788-9000, appearing for Plaintiff;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF WATCHANAPORN CHANGTO, SAKSEE ISANPAYU, ARUNROJ WIBOON, AKARAPHON SODSAENGSUK, JATTALEE CHALERNHINTHONG, WARITTHA RASANAKORN's MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT.

Plaintiffs' motion to strike the answer of Defendants 2001 Thai Stick, Inc. and Thai Stk, Inc. is granted without prejudice to Defendants filing a motion to set aside the defaults entered against them. On September 17, 2018, Defendants' defaults were entered. Such entry of default cut off Defendants' opportunity to file a responsive pleading. (Forbes v. Cameron Petroleums, Inc. (1978) 83 Cal.App.3d 257, 262 ["Since defendants' default had already been entered, they had no standing to file any responsive pleading without first obtaining relief from the default."]; see also Devlin v. Kearny Mesa AMC/Jeep/Renault, Inc. (1984) 155 Cal.App.3d 381, 385 ["The entry of a default terminates a defendant's rights to take any further affirmative steps in the litigation until either its default is set aside or a default judgment is entered."].) It follows that the Clerk of the Court erred in allowing Defendants to file their answer on March 25, 2019, and the answer must be stricken. Defendants may file a motion for relief from default.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
